data: Le tessere pubbliche dal tempio di Atena a Camarina
Federica Cordano, Le tessere pubbliche dal tempio di Atena a Camarina, Studi pubblicati dall’Istituto italiano per la storia antica, 50 (Roma, 1992)
The text is placed on both sides, at the top of the object.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Cordano, 1992, Le tessere pubbliche dal tempio di Atena a Camarina
Cordano, 1989, Primi documenti di un archivio anagrafico a Camarina
Text encoding: Valentina Mignosa, edition source:
The text is placed on one side, at the top of the object. On the b-side, it is possible to see letters that have been deliberately erased and are no longer legible.
The text is placed on both sides.
The text is set over 9 lines on one side.
Cassio, 1994, Giavellotti contro frecce. Una nuova lettura di una tessera dal tempio di Atena a Camarina e Hom. Od. 8,229
The text is placed on one side.
The text is placed on both sides of the object.
The text is placed on one side of the lamina.
The text is placed on both sides of the lamina.
Six fragments, one of which is larger. The other five are very small.
The text is on both sides of the lamina, which appears to have been used several times. Three different uses can be distinguished.

The text is placed on one side only of the lamina, which has been used twice. There are in fact two texts apparently 
                                        produced at two different times.
The text is placed on both sides of the lamina. On face b it has been deliberately erased and is no longer legible.
The text is placed on one side of the lamina and is almost entirely lost.
The text is placed on both sides of the lamina. On side b the text is deliberately erased and cannot be read.
The text is placed on both sides. Face b features two texts, made in two phases of use respectively.
The text is placed on both sides. Face a features two texts, made in two phases of use respectively.
The text is placed on both sides of the lamina. Face b features two texts, made in two phases of use respectively.
The text is placed on two sides of the lamina.
The text must have been in the part now lost of the tessera.
The text is placed on the two sides of the two fragments.
The lamina is twisted and the text unreadable.
The text is placed on one side of the lamina. On the other side (face b) there are traces of letters deliberately erased.

The text is placed on side b of the lamina; on side a there is a straight line. According to the editor, these lines were engraved on the lead
                                        sheet as a trace to cut out the tesserae.
The text is placed on one side of the lamina, fragmented in four non-contiguous pieces.
The text is placed on both sides of the lamina, which is twisted.
The text is placed on both sides of the two remaining fragments of the lamina.
The two fragments of the lamina are twisted and the text is unreadable.
The text is placed on both sides of the fragment.
The text is placed on both sides of the fragment of the lamina.
The text is placed on one side of one of the two fragments of the lamina.
The text is placed on the two sides of the lamina.
The text is not legible because the lamina has not been opened due to the poor state of preservation.
The text is placed on one side of the fragment.
The text was placed on both sides of the lamina but it has been erased on face b.
The text is placed on one side of the lamina, which is broken in two pieces after restoration.
The text is partly legible on one side of the lamina.
The text is partly legible on one side of the first and second fragments, on both sides of the third.
